summary: Verified backend persistence + GET round-trip and frontend popup rendering for delivery item packaging breakdown (UOM + bottle count). All 4 pytest checks pass; UI popup shows '1 × Crate-12 (12 bottles)' under the Qty column with the expected data-testid.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend conditional `item.packages > 0 && item.packaging_units > 1` (DistributorDetail.js:3710) correctly hides the breakdown for legacy items (no packages) and for single-bottle packs (packaging_units=1) — matches the spec.
DeliveryItem pydantic model exposes the three new fields as Optional, so legacy records without those keys deserialize without errors. Good defensive shape.
DistributorDetail.js is 4958 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting the delivery-detail dialog into its own component for maintainability (not blocking for this iteration).
seed_data_creation: Created and (best-effort) deleted draft delivery DEL-2026-0030 against distributor Brian (bb12d90e-4d33-4890-ac5f-17573c551b5c), Empire Restaurant account, Delhi warehouse, SKU 'Nyla – 330 ml / Silver', 1 × Crate-12 = 12 bottles. Pytest cleanup also runs DELETE; UI test left the draft in place for human re-verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file `test_iteration_265_delivery_packaging_breakdown.py` is idempotent: it reads distributor stock to size the bottle quantity, falling back to packaging_units=2 when stock <12. Brian has 2 active locations (one batch-tracked, one not); test picks the non-batch one. Required env: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL must be exported when running pytest from /app (root .env isn't auto-loaded).
rca of the issue: N/A — this was a feature verification, not a bug. Verified: (a) DeliveryItemCreate / DeliveryItem models carry packaging_type_name/packaging_units/packages (backend/models/distributor.py:509-511, 549-551); (b) create_delivery route persists those fields in distributor_delivery_items (routes/distributors.py:4808-4810); (c) get_delivery returns items raw including the new fields (line 4306-4311); (d) frontend payload sends packaging_type_name (DistributorDetail.js:1825); (e) popup renders the breakdown line at DistributorDetail.js:3710-3714 with data-testid='delivery-detail-item-packaging-0'. Confirmed end-to-end via API create→GET (quantity=12=1×12, packaging_type_name='Crate-12') and UI popup screenshot showing '1 × Crate-12 (12 bottles)'.
